description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:128-129 parses INTEGER r, then INTEGER s, and discards the remaining bytes ('let (s, _) = der_integer(rest)?'). It also slices the SEQUENCE body to seq_len at line 126 but never verifies the SEQUENCE consumed all of der (trailing bytes after the SEQUENCE are silently ignored at the top level too).

Trigger: a malformed signature blob with garbage appended after the SEQUENCE, or extra TLV elements inside the SEQUENCE after s.

Bad behavior: parses successfully and returns a (r||s) raw signature. The signature is still verifiable against the public key, so verification fails / succeeds correctly on the cryptographic level. But the parser is permissive in a way that diverges from the documented DER round-trip contract and would confuse anyone debugging an issue where AWS KMS returned trailing bytes (which would itself be a service-side bug worth surfacing rather than swallowing).

Fix: assert that der_integer's trailing 'rest' is empty after parsing s, AND that der_length consumed all of bytes after the SEQUENCE header.

Severity: P1 not P0 because there is no known trigger via AWS KMS (which produces strict DER) and there is no security impact (the resulting raw signature still has to verify against the public key). But this is the kind of laxity that hides upstream-protocol drift bugs.
title: ecdsa_der_to_raw_p256 silently ignores trailing bytes after the second INTEGER
